title: Act II Finish — Pool, Drawing Room, Doritos, Peacock

references.@Image3: Chunk 3 storyboard grid — pool, gallery, drawing room, product, peacock beats

prompt:
@Image1's character as the subject, gold wrap dress throughout. Scene and color grade reference @Image2. Shot sequence reference @Image3. Prestige commercial finish, deadpan surreal, 15 seconds. Doritos appear only final four seconds. No steak anywhere.

0–4s: Wide indoor pool conservatory steamy glass walls turquoise water brass ladder, actress stands fully dressed at marble pool edge talks to camera, white swan glides across water behind she does not react, soft water lap dialogue on pool boy and boundaries.

4–7s: Wide gallery hall dark wood crystal chandelier red runner, actress walks toward camera deadpan delivery on chlorine and hope, smooth backward track centred.

7–10s: Medium drawing room white marble fireplace gilt mantel, stern older woman oil portrait in heavy gilt frame on wall behind her shoulder, actress stands centre facing camera finishes monologue on mother in frame and exits does not glance at portrait, slow push in dialogue ends at 10s.

10–12s: Same drawing room, butler enters from right silver platter with opened Nacho Cheese Doritos bag centred on tray, actress reaches lifts one chip without breaking eye contact, silver tray clink no dialogue.

12–13s: Close-up actress bites Dorito loud crisp crunch expression unchanged, room tone dips.

13–15s: Wide drawing room white peacock full tail struts foreground left to right herringbone parquet, actress by fireplace mid-ground chewing does not look at peacock, hard cut extreme close-up she says one deadpan word Bold, super title Doritos For the Bold.

Voice: posh British monotone through 10s, silence during crunch, one word at 14s. She never names Doritos in dialogue.

Dialogue: The pool boy left a note about pH. I left one about boundaries. He cried. Men do. I don't. Before six I don't do chlorine, or hope, or subtitles. My mother says I'm dramatic. She's in a frame. Not mine. I don't collect guilt. I collect exits.

style.look: prestige commercial finish, Doritos appear only in final four seconds of picture
style.mood: deadpan throughout, product and peacock completely unacknowledged
style.pacing: monologue completes before product enters frame

dialogue.voice: same posh British monotone through 0:10, silence during crunch, one deadpan word at 0:14
dialogue.text: The pool boy left a note about pH. I left one about boundaries. He cried. Men do. I don't. Before six I don't do chlorine, or hope, or subtitles. My mother says I'm dramatic. She's in a frame. Not mine. I don't collect guilt. I collect exits.

product.name: Doritos Nacho Cheese
product.appearance: opened Nacho Cheese Doritos bag centred on silver platter, triangular chip in her hand at bite
product.rule: butler and bag enter at 0:10 only, actress never names product in dialogue

segments.audio: soft water lap, distant room echo, dialogue on pool boy and boundaries

segments.scene: gallery hall, dark wood panelling, crystal chandelier, red runner carpet

segments.camera: smooth backward track keeping her centred in hallway

segments.audio: quiet drawing room tone, dialogue ends at 0:10

segments.audio: silver tray faint clink, no dialogue

segments.audio: LOUD Dorito crunch, room tone dips under crunch

segments.audio: soft peacock call, actress says 'Bold.', super title sting
